title: Michael Kerr
text: I am a computational geographer specializing in high-performance computing and machine learning for complex spatial systems. My current research is on spatial statistics, optimization, and algorithmic equity. I aim to identify robust, scale-invariant patterns in environmental data. As I transition to graduate research, my focus is shifting toward geospatial computer vision and scaling neural rendering models (NeRFs) for real-time edge hardware. I am also investigating how dynamical systems, PDEs, and tensor methods can be used to develop interpretable, physics-informed machine learning models. My goal is to translate advanced mathematical theory into practical, sustainable strategies.

title: Michael Kerr
text:
Research Interests

Computer Vision & Edge Computing
Spatial Optimization & Algorithmic Fairness
Topological Data Analysis (TDA) & Spatial Statistics
Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) & Tensor Decomposition

title: Michael Kerr
